title: davallia pumila
intro: Davallia pumila, also known as the Japanese maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Davalliaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Davallia pumila include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, Davallia pumila is typically dried and used as a decoction or infusion to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
